rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RODNEY BRUCE MIDDLETON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues: The caption of the petition does not disclose all relief sought. [CRC 7.102.] Costs advanced are not clearly supported by the five invoices attached as Exhibit D. The attorney statutory fee is slightly miscalculated. The petition does not set forth in detail how the estate is to be distributed (including the legal description of the real property), and there is no mention of the tangible personal property. [CRC 7.651 and LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GEORGIA E HUNT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/05/2022. 1) The court requires an amended petition that includes the request under Probate Code 21380 creating a presumption of fraud or undue influence in the caption of the petition and in the new notice. 2) Please provide the legal basis/cite legal authority for distribution of the ring and watch to Nancy Hunt. 3) Please amend the petition to include a pro rata payment of the $800.00 assessment fee. 4) Please provide proof the original Will has been lodged or explain why the Will has not been lodged. [Probate Code 8200] 5) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/20/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Close Of The Estate
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required to explain the delay in filing the I & A, due 4 months after letters issued (by 6/19/21). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CYNTHIA COURTNEY REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST OF 2000, dated MAY 15, 2000
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition (1) To Recover Assets Of Trust And Estate. (2) Recovery Of Damages And Attrorneys Fees Under Probate Code Section 859. (3) For Breac
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/24/22. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] No courtesy copy of the proof of service was received by the assigned Examiner, but a quick skim of it online indicates that the wrong form of Notice of Hearing was served. There is no copy of the trust attached to the petition and the interested parties are not identified in the petition, so the Court cannot determine if notice was properly served on the appropriate parties. A supplement to the petition must be filed to provide the missing documents to the extent they are available to the petitioner and to identify the interested parties and their addresses, in any event. Notice of the supplement with a copy of it must also be served on the interested parties after it has been filed. [CRC 7.53(b).] A courtesy copy of the supplement, the proof of service of the correct Notice of Hearing (showing all pleadings filed and to be heard), and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/14/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YUNG CHIH YUEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to January 5, 2022. waiver of notice by an heir does not satisfy the requirement to give notice to the consulate, required when an heir is a citizen of a different country. in addition, the declaration must be signed and verified by the petitioner. the exception that allows an attorney to verify on behalf of a client does not apply when the client is petitioning to be appointed a fiduciary in the case (see CRC 7.103(c)).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHERINE CAVNESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/04/2022. 1) Please explain how much bond is being requested as page 1 #2 d. (2) requests $1200.00.00 in bond. Total value of property on page 2 #3.d. 7 appears to be incorrect. 2) Petitioner has not checked a required box on page 3 # 5.a. (3) or (4). 3) The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D.] 4) Petitioner must file Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). 5) Petitioner must file proof of publication. 6) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/20/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILSON PENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HIDEO IWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: grant without hearing. bond ordered of $631,000. close of estate set for 12/26/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WALKER SCANLAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 12/22/2022. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 01/09/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOUISE C. AHERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 12/19/2022. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 12/19/2022. Appearance will be required on 12/19/2022 if no petition has been filed.
